root_text.sn38.6:1.2: Kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, paramassāsappatto hotī”ti?
root_text.sn38.6:1.3: “Yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhu channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ viditvā anupādāvimutto hoti, ettāvatā kho, āvuso, paramassāsappatto hotī”ti.
root_text.sn38.6:1.4: “Atthi panāvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etassa paramassāsassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.6:1.5: “Atthi kho, āvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etassa paramassāsassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.6:1.6: “Katamo pana, āvuso, maggo katamā paṭipadā, etassa paramassāsassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.6:1.7: “Ayameva kho, āvuso, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo etassa paramassāsassa sacchikiriyāya, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn38.6:1.9: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, maggo ayaṁ paṭipadā, etassa paramassāsassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.6:1.10: “Bhaddako, āvuso, maggo bhaddikā paṭipadā, etassa paramassāsassa sacchikiriyāya.

translation_text.sn38.6:1.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘gaining ultimate solace’.
translation_text.sn38.6:1.2: At what point do you gain ultimate solace?”
translation_text.sn38.6:1.3: “When a mendicant is freed by not grasping after truly understanding the six fields of contact’s origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape, at that point they’ve gained ultimate solace.”
translation_text.sn38.6:1.4: “But, reverend, is there a path and a practice for realizing this ultimate solace?”
